source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 9. TCF_ADD(HAD1417MCW) 221215 (Rev.4) - PMS Update/Attachment 05. Safety Test Report/397950 CBTC-TR H&abyz ADD (Basic)_Consolidated.pdf

document.title: IEC 60601-1 Test Report No. 269183
document.documentType: Safety test report / IEC 60601-1 CB test report extract
document.revisionOrVersion: TRF No. IEC60601_1J; IEC 60601-1 3rd edition national deviations attachments

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk covers IEC 60601-1 safety testing and national deviations for a DEPS9601 power supply unit, including temperature, fire, leakage current, dielectric strength, single fault, critical component, and Canada/Japan/Korea/Switzerland deviation results.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory technical-file evidence for basic safety conformity of the power supply component, with multiple items requiring additional evaluation in the final end-product application.
keyPoints: Power supply unit was repeatedly marked for additional evaluation in the end product application for applied-parts, mechanical, ME system, EMC, and several construction items.
Measured power input did not exceed 110% of ratings; rated input stated as 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz.
Protective earth impedance, leakage current, dielectric strength, ball pressure, excessive temperature, transformer overheating, and single fault condition tables show pass results.
Critical components table identifies certified or equipment-evaluated mains, insulation, capacitors, transformers, PCB, connectors, fuses, switches, and protective parts.
National deviation attachments include Canadian, Japanese, Korean, and Swiss regulatory differences, with several items passed or noted for end-product evaluation.

entities.name: DEPS9601
entities.evidence: 11.1.1 temperature table lists Model No.: DEPS9601; multiple clauses state the equipment is a power supply unit.

entities.name: Report No. 269183
entities.evidence: Page headers repeatedly show Report No. 269183 for IEC 60601-1.

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Each report page is headed IEC 60601-1 with Clause Requirement + Test Result - Remark Verdict.

entities.name: CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08
entities.evidence: Attachment 2 states Canadian National Differences according to Canadian National standard: CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08.

entities.name: JIS T0601-1:2012
entities.evidence: Japan National Differences are stated according to JIS T0601-1:2012 (IEC 60601-1:2005 Ed. 3 (MOD)).

entities.name: KS C IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Korea National Differences are stated according to KS C IEC 60601-1.

entities.name: IECEE
entities.evidence: National deviation attachments include copyright notice for IEC System for Conformity Testing and Certification of Electrical Equipment (IECEE).

concepts.name: Excessive temperature protection
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.1.1 passed; appended tables list measured component and enclosure temperatures under 90 V/60 Hz and 264 V/50 Hz conditions.

concepts.name: Fire prevention and fire enclosure construction
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.2 Fire prevention and Clause 11.3 fire enclosure construction show pass results for enclosure strength, internal wire rating, PCB flammability, and no bottom openings.

concepts.name: Leakage current compliance
concepts.evidence: 8.7 leakage current tables state measured leakage currents did not exceed allowable limits for earth leakage and touch current in normal and single fault conditions.

concepts.name: Dielectric strength / MOOP insulation
concepts.evidence: 8.8.3 dielectric strength table lists 1 MOOP and 2 MOOP insulation tests with no dielectric breakdown after one minute.

concepts.name: Single fault condition testing
concepts.evidence: 13.2 tables list short-circuit, overload, and blocked-opening tests with hazardous situation marked NO.

concepts.name: Critical component control
concepts.evidence: 8.10 critical component tables identify component manufacturers, ratings, applicable standards, and conformity marks or equipment evaluation status.

concepts.name: End-product evaluation dependency
concepts.evidence: Multiple clauses state the equipment is a power supply unit and shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application.

evidence.claim: The DEPS9601 power supply passed IEC 60601-1 excessive temperature evaluation for relevant ME equipment parts in this chunk.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.1.1 verdict P; tables for Model No. DEPS9601 show measured temperatures corrected to 40 °C under 90 V/60 Hz and 264 V/50 Hz, with enclosure hot spots below 56 °C limits.

evidence.claim: The equipment is a power supply unit and several safety aspects require additional evaluation in the final end product.
evidence.evidenceText: Remarks repeatedly state: The equipment is a power supply unit, shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application.

evidence.claim: Power input testing passed and measured input current did not exceed 110% of unit ratings.
evidence.evidenceText: 4.11 Power Input table verdict P; supplementary information states measured input current did not exceed 110% of ratings; rated 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz.

evidence.claim: Marking legibility and durability testing passed.
evidence.evidenceText: 7.1.2 Legibility of Marking verdict P with outside markings and symbols clearly legible; 7.1.3 durability test verdict P with label no curling/loosening and ink remained legible after water, ethanol, and isopropyl alcohol rubbing.

evidence.claim: Residual voltage on mains plug pins after disconnection complied with the 60 V maximum allowable limit.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.4.3 table verdict P; maximum allowable voltage 60 V; measured values between plug pins and earth pin were 0 V after disconnection at 264 V, 60 Hz.

evidence.claim: Protective earth connection impedance passed the IEC 60601-1 limits.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.6.4 table verdict P; appliance inlet ground pin to potential equalization connector measured 0.019 ohm and to metal enclosure 0.012 ohm against 100 milliohm allowable impedance.

evidence.claim: Earth leakage current complied in normal and single fault conditions, including before and after humidity conditioning.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.7 table verdict P; earth leakage measurements at 264 V/60 Hz ranged up to 572 uA against 5 mA NC and 10 mA SFC limits; supplementary information states measured leakage currents did not exceed allowable limits.

evidence.claim: Touch current complied in normal and single fault conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.7 touch current table verdict P; maximum listed touch current was 287 uA under SFC against a 500 uA limit, and NC values were 2-6 uA against a 100 uA limit.

evidence.claim: Solid insulation dielectric strength testing showed no breakdown for listed MOOP insulation barriers.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.8.3 table verdict P; insulation areas A through E tested at 1500 V, 1532 V, 3000 V, or 16 Vdc as applicable, with dielectric breakdown marked No.

evidence.claim: Thermoplastic ball pressure testing passed for representative connector and core cap materials.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.8.4.1 table verdict P; allowed impression diameter <= 2 mm, measured 1.3 mm for PBT core cap and 1.2 mm for Nylon AC connector male/female at 125 °C.

evidence.claim: Fire prevention and fire enclosure construction passed for evaluated component-power-supply aspects.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.2 Fire prevention verdict P; enclosure strength passed. Clause 11.3 passed for internal wire rating, PCB flammability minimum V-1, and no bottom openings below fire enclosure.

evidence.claim: The unit is not intended for oxygen-rich environments, flammable anesthetics, flammable agents, sterilization, or liquid handling.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 11.2.2, 11.4, 11.5, 11.6.2, 11.6.3, and 11.6.7 state not intended for oxygen rich environments, flammable anesthetics/agents, reservoirs/liquids, or sterilization.

evidence.claim: Interruption and restoration of power supply did not result in loss of basic safety or essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.8 states no hazard associated with interruption of power and verdict P.

evidence.claim: The equipment has no programmable electrical medical system, alarm system, batteries, heating elements, motors, applied parts, or thermal cut-outs in this evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 12.3, 13.2.13, 14.1, 15.4.2, and 15.4.3 state no alarm systems, no heating element/motor, no programmable electrical medical system, no thermal cut-out, and no batteries.

evidence.claim: Single fault electrical short-circuit testing did not create hazardous situations.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.2 tables list short circuits including +16 Vdc output to accessible metal enclosure, C13, PC1/PC2, C8, C5, U4, U1, and T1; hazardous situation column shows NO.

evidence.claim: A +16 Vdc output overload test did not create a hazardous situation.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.2 table records +16 Vdc output overload at input 264 V for 4 h 20 min, DC output overload 3.60 A, T1 winding stabilized at 83.1 °C, no hazard, hazardous situation NO.

evidence.claim: Blocked ventilation/openings testing did not create a hazardous situation.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.2.7 impairment of cooling: all openings blocked, input 90 V, 1.07 A, 1 h 4 min; normal operated, transformer T1 coil 94.5 °C, no hazard, hazardous situation NO.

evidence.claim: Critical components were documented with standards, ratings, and certification marks or equipment evaluation status.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.10 critical components list includes appliance inlet Schurter 6220 to IEC 60320-1/UL 498, fuses ORISEL 55T to IEC 60127-2/UL 248-1, Y and X capacitors to IEC/UL capacitor standards, transformer T1, PCB, opto-couplers, and connectors.

evidence.claim: Canada national deviations were included and component and mains fuse requirements passed by reference to critical components table.
evidence.evidenceText: Canadian attachment states CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08; clauses 4.8 and 8.11.5 reference appended Table 8.10 in main test report No. 269183 with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Japan national deviations were included for JIS T0601-1:2012, with several insulation and dielectric strength differences passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Japan attachment states differences according to JIS T0601-1:2012; clauses 8.8.2 and 8.8.3 reference main report No. 269183/tested accordingly with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Korea national deviations accepted the supply rating and frequency range.
evidence.evidenceText: Korea national differences state national supply voltages 110, 220 V and 380 V; equipment rated 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz and verdict P; frequency requirement including 60 Hz verdict P.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 5 of 6; document-level title, date, and full product identification may appear outside this chunk.
Many clauses are marked N/A because the evaluated article is a power supply unit; multiple remarks require additional evaluation in the final end-product application.
Document date is not visible in this chunk; revisionOrVersion is limited to the visible TRF/national-deviation edition references.
